raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The paper opens as a viewpoint-generalization method ('first method designed to maintain accuracy under arbitrary camera viewpoint shifts') but closes as an augmentation recipe; the thesis and contribution claim require reconciliation regardless of improvement depth.","R1.2":"Introduction premises (deployment-time viewpoint robustness) and conclusions ('simple but effective addition to the standard training recipe') are incoherent; the argument never connects homography augmentation to cross-viewpoint transfer.","R1.3":"All reported evidence is in-distribution accuracy (Cityscapes-val, BDD100K-val mIoU); there are no cross-viewpoint evaluations to substantiate the primary advertised claim, making evidence-claim alignment a central concern.","R1.4":"Homography and perspective augmentation for segmentation are not novel; the 'first method' claim needs grounding against domain-adaptation, viewpoint-invariant, and augmentation-strategy literature.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' inherently encompasses sentence and paragraph quality; the closing paragraph is also notably repetitive and weakens the paper's ending."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the improvements address substantive issues such as the misalignment between the viewpoint-generalization framing and the standard-benchmark-only evaluation, or are you seeking surface-level writing and clarity improvements only?"}

rationale.R1.1: The paper opens as a viewpoint-generalization method ('first method designed to maintain accuracy under arbitrary camera viewpoint shifts') but closes as an augmentation recipe; the thesis and contribution claim require reconciliation regardless of improvement depth.
rationale.R1.2: Introduction premises (deployment-time viewpoint robustness) and conclusions ('simple but effective addition to the standard training recipe') are incoherent; the argument never connects homography augmentation to cross-viewpoint transfer.
rationale.R1.3: All reported evidence is in-distribution accuracy (Cityscapes-val, BDD100K-val mIoU); there are no cross-viewpoint evaluations to substantiate the primary advertised claim, making evidence-claim alignment a central concern.
rationale.R1.4: Homography and perspective augmentation for segmentation are not novel; the 'first method' claim needs grounding against domain-adaptation, viewpoint-invariant, and augmentation-strategy literature.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' inherently encompasses sentence and paragraph quality; the closing paragraph is also notably repetitive and weakens the paper's ending.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvements address substantive issues such as the misalignment between the viewpoint-generalization framing and the standard-benchmark-only evaluation, or are you seeking surface-level writing and clarity improvements only?
